name: Bread Pudding (Commander's Palace Restaurant)
recipeIngredient: 5 tbsp Melted and cooled unsalted butter
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Arrange bread in a 9-inch square baking pan.
3. Beat together remaining pudding ingredients in a mixing bowl and pour over bread. Let sit for 5 minutes, then turn bread over and let soak for another 10 minutes.
4. Cover pan with aluminum foil and place in a larger shallow pan. Fill the outer pan with hot water so it comes halfway up the side of the baking pan. Bake for 35 minutes, then remove foil and bake for another 10-15 minutes. Custard should still be somewhat soft with a light brown top.
5. Optional: Heat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit. Butter and lightly sugar a 2 1/2 quart souffle dish. Refrigerate until ready to use. Put the egg yolks and granulated sugar in the top of a double boiler and cook, whisking constantly, until the mixture is thick and lemon colored. In a large bowl, combine the yolk mixture with 2 1/2 cups of the cooled bread pudding. Beat the egg whites with a pinch of salt until frothy. Gradually add the confectioners sugar, beating constantly until the mixture forms stiff peaks. Add 1/2 cup of the beaten egg whites to the bread pudding-egg yolk mixture and gently fold the remaining egg whites into the bread pudding mixture. Fill the souffle dish 3/4 full with the bread pudding mixture and bake for 30-35 minutes until the top is golden brown. Serve warm.
